title: Grease on Leather
timeEstimate: Pro-lane; home is powder + condition only
stainChemistry: "Grease" on leather covers thick fats — kitchen grease, cooking-pan splatter, food drippings. Heavier and more adherent than thin cooking oil; slower to penetrate but harder to lift once set. On leather, grease that gets past the topcoat polymerizes over time into a darker shadow that's nearly impossible to remove without re-dyeing. Same leather-banned-tools list as cooking-oil-leather: NO household solvents (acetone / alcohol / mineral spirits — destroy finish), NO oxidizers (peroxide / OxiClean / chlorine — strip finish + dye + oils). For motor oil / mechanical grease, see motor-oil-* cards. Pro uses VDS at controlled dilution + immediate conditioning.
whyThisWorks: Cornstarch / fuller's earth preload pulls surface grease up via capillary action — works even better on thick grease than on thin oil because higher surface tension gives powder more to grab. Mild soap handles surface residue. Pro VDS for residual penetrated grease. Conditioner restores hide.
defaultAssumption: Finished leather assumed; aniline = pro from minute one. Most leather furniture / handbag / car interior is finished. Suede: separate card. For motor oil specifically, see motor-oil-* cards (different chemistry).

spottingProtocol.instruction: Water-drop test: beads = finished, soaks = aniline (pro from minute one). If grease is solid (cooled bacon grease, hardened fat), lift bulk with the FLAT edge of a plastic card. No scraping leather.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cornstarch, baking soda, or fuller's earth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cover with thick layer of powder. 30–60 min absorb. Soft brush off. Repeat with fresh powder if first saturated.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 1–2 drops Dawn in warm water, white cloth wrung nearly dry
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dawn in warm water, wring cloth nearly dry. Wipe outside-in. Plain-water damp wipe to clear soap.
Pro applies VDS to a white cloth, controlled tamp on residual penetrated grease. Strict dilution + immediate conditioning.
Mandatory after any wet or solvent treatment.

steps: 1. Water-drop leather test. Aniline = pro from minute one.
2. Lift solid grease bulk with flat-edge plastic card.
3. Cornstarch / fuller's earth preload 30–60 min. Brush off.
4. Mild soap on barely-damp cloth, outside-in.
5. Plain-water damp wipe to clear soap.
professionalProtocol.temperature: ambient — no heat (heat dries leather + polymerizes grease)
warnings: NEVER hydrogen peroxide / OxiClean / chlorine — strip leather permanently
NEVER acetone / undiluted alcohol / mineral spirits at home
Aniline = pro from minute one (grease soaks straight in)

homeSolutions.agent: Identify Your Leather First — One Drop of Water
homeSolutions.instruction: Water-drop test on hidden leather: beads = finished, soaks in = aniline (STOP, pro from minute one). Aniline absorbs grease directly into the dyed hide and home cleaning won't reach it.

The internet's worst leather-grease advice: WD-40, mineral spirits, acetone, peroxide, OxiClean. ALL of those strip the leather's finish + dye + oils, permanently. The damage is worse than the grease. Stick to powder + mild soap + condition.

homeSolutions.agent: Lift Solid Bulk — Flat Edge of Plastic Card
homeSolutions.instruction: If the grease is solid (cooled bacon grease, hardened fat), use the FLAT edge of a plastic card to lift it off. Don't scrape leather — flat-edge lift only. Most surface bulk comes off.

Cover the grease spot with cornstarch (or baking soda, or fuller's earth — fuller's earth works best on thick grease). 30–60 min absorb. Soft brush off. Repeat with fresh layer if the first looks saturated.

homeSolutions.agent: Mild Soap on a BARELY-Damp Cloth
homeSolutions.instruction: 1 to 2 drops of Dawn in warm water. Dampen a clean white cloth, WRING IT OUT until barely damp. Wipe outside-in. Plain-water-damp wipe to clear soap. Leather should never look visibly wet.
Apply leather conditioner (Lexol or Bickmore) per the label. Cleaning + grease both strip natural oils — conditioning prevents cracking.

homeSolutions.agent: Pro for Anything Remaining — What to Tell Them
homeSolutions.instruction: Take to a leather care specialist. Tell them: "Grease on [finished / aniline / unknown] leather, age [hours / days], one home pass with cornstarch preload + mild soap + conditioner. No oxidizer, no acetone, no alcohol, no mineral spirits, no WD-40 used."

steps: 1. Water-drop test. Aniline = pro from minute one.
2. NO oxidizer, NO acetone, NO alcohol, NO mineral spirits, NO WD-40.
3. Lift solid grease with flat-edge plastic card.
5. Mild soap (1–2 drops Dawn) on a BARELY-damp cloth, outside-in.
6. Plain-water damp wipe to clear soap.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if leather is aniline, if grease remains after powder + mild soap, or if anyone has applied harsh chemistry previously.
warnings: NEVER WD-40 / acetone / alcohol / mineral spirits on leather
For motor oil specifically, see motor-oil-* cards

neverDo: Never WD-40 / acetone / alcohol / mineral spirits on leather
fiberSensitivities: Finished / pigmented leather: tolerates cornstarch + mild soap + conditioner
Suede: own protocol (no liquid, no powder paste)

materialWarnings: Cornstarch + mild soap + condition is the safe home path
Conditioner after every wet treatment, mandatory

escalation.when: Aniline / unfinished leather (always), grease remaining after powder + mild soap pass, or anyone has applied harsh chemistry previously.
escalation.whatToTell: "Grease on [finished / aniline / unknown] leather, age [hours / days], one home pass with cornstarch preload + mild soap + conditioner. No oxidizer, no acetone, no alcohol, no mineral spirits, no WD-40 used."
escalation.specialistType: leather care specialist (NOT a regular dry cleaner)

contextQuestions: Water-drop test: bead, soak, or in-between?
Type of grease — kitchen / cooking pan / engine?
Has anyone applied solvent / oxidizer / WD-40?
How long ago — fresh or aged?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Motor oil = different protocol (see motor-oil-* cards). Aged grease that polymerized into the hide leaves a near-permanent shadow. Aniline absorbs grease directly — pro from minute one.
